# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { VercelRequest, VercelResponse } from '@vercel/node';
// Assuming that the feature flags are in a separate file named featuresConfig 
require('dotenv').config(); // Importing dotenv to handle env variables. You may have them as .env files too if you want more granular access control over your keys and database urls
import { config } from './featuresConfig';   
const API_KEY = process.env['EDGE_CONFIG'] || 'vercel_default_key_789' ;  // This line will default to a value once the .ENV file is not defined (the variable must be set in runtime) and then overwrite that with EDGE_CONF
const DB_URL = config.databaseUrl;  
// feature flags are simply enabled or disabled via environment variables using process envs 
export const edgeFunction  = async(req: VercelRequest, res :VercelResponse): Promise<void> => {    // Typing is done with the generic parameter 'any'. You might need to add type definitions based on your actual requirements. For now assuming it as promise-based response handler 
   if (config[API_KEY]) {     } else{      throw new Error('Invalid API KEY');}       const result = await fetch(DB_URL); // Performing database operation here using fetched api key and db url, actual implementation is left to you.    };// end of asyn function
